description:
The SecretStore, WritableSecretStore, and SigningBackend implementations on Tpm2Backend have no doc comments on their methods. The trait-level docs in secretx-core describe the general contract, but TPM-specific preconditions are only discoverable by reading the crate-level module docs or the source:

- get()/put() only work in NV mode (error if called in Sign mode)
- sign()/public_key_der() only work in Sign mode (error if called in NV mode)
- All operations require null-auth (empty owner-hierarchy authorization)
- sign() only works with unrestricted signing keys (restricted keys get TPM_RC_TICKET)
- NV operations are single-shot, limited by MAX_NV_BUFFER_SIZE
- Each call opens a fresh ESAPI context (no connection pooling)

What goes wrong: A caller reads the trait docs, constructs a Tpm2Backend, and calls sign() with a restricted key. They get a raw TPM error with no explanation. The crate-level docs mention this, but rustdoc for the method itself shows only the trait-level docs.

Locations: lib.rs lines 255-308 (SecretStore impl), 312-358 (WritableSecretStore impl), 362-486 (SigningBackend impl).
title: Trait impl methods lack TPM-specific doc comments
